MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Mar-14-2016 Assigned To Trial Court

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHAMPION INDUSTRIAL CONTRACTORS, INC.''S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Kimberly Chu of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and opposing party; Josh S. Sullivan of Foley & Mansfield (510.590.9520) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issue Three (premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to Issues One and Two (negligence and products liability). As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issue Three, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Raymond Townsend was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant''s failure to address decedent''''s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Decedent''s deposition testimony would be and is admissible because decedent is unavailable, defendant was a party to decedent''s personal injury action and defendant had the right and opportunity to cross-examine decedent with an interest and motive similar to that which it has in this action. (Evid. Code 1291.) However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, decedent''s deposition testimony and the declaration of Charles Ay would create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. In that case, defendant''s objections to decedent''s deposition testimony and to Mr. Ay''s declaration would be overruled. Summary adjudication of Issues One and Two is taken off calendar as plaintiffs have dismissed their negligence and products liability causes of action. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Angela Sinclair, CSR 13902 of Aiken Welch (877.451.1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Dorothy Townsend, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BROWN SHEET METAL & MECHANICAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Brown Sheet Metal & Mechanical, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. The Court considered defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication as defendant's separate statement complied with CRC 3.1350.
As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issues One and Three (negligence), defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Raymond Townsend was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's failure to address decedent's deposition testimony and to address properly identified person with knowledge Charlie Hedden precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Decedent's deposition testimony would be and is admissible because decedent is unavailable, defendant was a party to decedent's personal injury action and defendant had the right and opportunity to cross-examine decedent with an interest and motive similar to that which it has in this action. (Evid. Code 1291.) Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to Michael Brown's declaration are overruled as to paragraphs 1 and 2 and sustained as to paragraph 10. However even if Mr. Brown's declaration was entirely admissible, it would be insufficient to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim.
As to summary adjudication Issues Two and Four (strict liability), defendant's separate statement fails to demonstrate any facts relevant to these causes of action and thus defendant failed to sustain its initial burden.
As to summary adjudication Issue Five (premise owner/contractor liability), defendant's separate statement fails to demonstrate any facts to support the ground upon which defendant moved, i.e., that there's no evidence that defendant retained control over decedent's job site, and thus defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Further, defendant failed to demonstrate that this cause of action fails even if defendant did not retain control over decedent's job site. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
